description: Score and track commit message quality over time with trend analysis and revert correlation

userStories.title: Scoring algorithm
userStories.description: As a developer I want each commit message scored 0–100 so I can objectively measure quality.
acceptanceCriteria: score_message(msg: &str) -> u8 returns 0–100
Empty string scores 0
Single-word messages ('fix', 'wip', 'update') score ≤ 20
Conventional commit format (type(scope): description) scores ≥ 70
Message with body (blank line after subject) earns +10 bonus capped at 100
Subject > 72 chars penalised -10
Subject < 10 chars penalised -20
Pure lowercase subject with no punctuation penalised -5
Merge commits (message starts with 'Merge ') score 50 fixed (exempt from vague detection)
Score is deterministic — same input always same output
Unit tests cover all scoring branches in tests/commit_quality.rs

userStories.title: Vague message detection
userStories.description: As a developer I want vague commit messages flagged by pattern so I know which ones to improve.
acceptanceCriteria: is_vague(msg: &str) -> bool returns true for known vague patterns
Vague patterns (case-insensitive): 'wip', 'fix', 'fixes', 'fixed', 'update', 'updates', 'updated', 'misc', 'stuff', 'changes', 'cleanup', 'refactor', 'test', 'tests', 'temp', 'tmp', 'asdf', '...'
Match is whole-message after trimming (not substring match) unless message is ≤ 3 words
Non-English messages (detected via non-ASCII majority) are not flagged as vague
Merge commit messages are never flagged as vague
is_vague returns false for any message scoring ≥ 50
Unit tests cover all pattern variants

userStories.title: DB storage for scores
userStories.description: As the system I want scores stored in SQLite so they persist and can be queried over time.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration adds commit_quality table: id, repo_path, commit_hash, score INTEGER, is_vague INTEGER, scored_at TEXT
UNIQUE INDEX on (repo_path, commit_hash) — no double-scoring
insert_commit_quality(conn, repo_path, commit_hash, score, is_vague) -> anyhow::Result<bool> in db.rs
Returns false on duplicate (INSERT OR IGNORE semantics)
score_and_store called from git_ops.rs poll_repo() for every new commit event (event_type='commit'), not merges
Existing commits already in git_activity but not in commit_quality are backfilled on first poll (best-effort, capped at 200)
Migration is additive — existing DB opens without data loss
Integration test: insert activity, open DB, verify quality row present

userStories.title: Trend calculation
userStories.description: As a developer I want weekly average scores so I can see if my commit quality is improving.
acceptanceCriteria: commit_quality_trend(conn, weeks: u32) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<WeeklyQuality>> in query.rs
WeeklyQuality { week_start: NaiveDate, commit_count: usize, avg_score: f64, vague_count: usize, vague_pct: f64 }
weeks param controls how many calendar weeks back to include (1–52)
Week boundaries use Monday-midnight local time (consistent with week_range())
Weeks with zero scored commits produce a row with avg_score=0.0, vague_count=0
Only 'commit' event_type rows are included (not merges, not branch_switch)
Unit test: insert known scores across 3 weeks, verify trend output

userStories.title: Revert correlation
userStories.description: As a developer I want to see if commits that got reverted had lower quality scores so I can learn from patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: find_reverted_commits(conn) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<RevertedCommit>> in query.rs
RevertedCommit { original_hash: String, original_message: String, revert_hash: String, score: Option<u8>, repo_path: String }
Detection: commit message matches regex '^[Rr]evert .+' and message body contains 'This reverts commit <hash>'
Extracts original_hash from the revert message body (first 40-char hex string)
Looks up score for original_hash in commit_quality table
If original not in commit_quality, score is None
Returns empty vec if no reverts found — no error
Unit test: seed revert commit in git_activity, seed score, verify correlation result

userStories.title: CLI command
userStories.description: As a developer I want a blackbox commit-quality command to see my scores and trends.
acceptanceCriteria: New subcommand: Commands::CommitQuality { weeks: u32, format: OutputFormat, show_reverts: bool } in cli.rs
Default weeks=8, default format=pretty, show_reverts=false
--weeks N flag controls trend window (1–52, validated by clap range)
--show-reverts flag enables revert correlation section
Command registered in main.rs match arm
Uses existing load_config / open_db pattern from main.rs
CLI integration test: cargo run -- commit-quality exits 0 with empty DB

userStories.title: Pretty output renderer
userStories.description: As a developer I want readable terminal output showing trend table and flagged vague commits.
acceptanceCriteria: render_commit_quality(trend: &[WeeklyQuality], reverts: &[RevertedCommit], format: &OutputFormat) -> String in output.rs
Pretty mode: header '=== Commit Message Quality ===' in bold/cyan
Pretty mode: trend table with columns Week | Commits | Avg Score | Vague | Vague%
Pretty mode: avg score coloured green ≥70, yellow 40–69, red <40
Pretty mode: vague% coloured red if >20%
Pretty mode: if show_reverts and non-empty, section 'Reverted Commits' listing hash, original score, message
Pretty mode: if no scored commits in range, print 'No scored commits in the last N weeks.'
JSON mode: serialise WeeklyQuality vec + reverts as { trend: [...], reverts: [...] }
CSV mode: flat rows with columns week_start, commit_count, avg_score, vague_count, vague_pct
render_commit_quality_to_string used in unit tests (no stdout side effects)
Unit test: known WeeklyQuality slice produces expected pretty string

userStories.title: Integration tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer I want integration tests so regressions are caught in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/commit_quality.rs covers: score_message variants, is_vague patterns, insert_commit_quality dedup, commit_quality_trend with multi-week data, find_reverted_commits
assert_cmd test: blackbox commit-quality exits 0 with temp DB
assert_cmd test: blackbox commit-quality --weeks 4 --format json exits 0 and stdout is valid JSON
All tests pass: cargo test
No clippy warnings: cargo clippy -- -D warnings
No test uses std::env::set_var without unsafe block (Rust 2024 rule)

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
